sourceMovie.title: Leprechaun
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Leprechaun (1993) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Evil Dead II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Leprechaun clicked for you, Evil Dead II (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Leprechaun clicked for you, Hot Fuzz (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Leprechaun?
faq.answer: Try Evil Dead II, Freaky, Fright Night Part 2—each shares DNA with Leprechaun while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Leprechaun?
faq.answer: Leprechaun (1993) is best described as Horror, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Leprechaun part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Leprechaun Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
